questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is AWS's object storage service designed for storing and retrieving any amount of data (e.g., files, backups, logs).
questions.question: In AWS, which service is primarily used for object storage of files like images, backups, and logs?
questions.topic: Aws Core Services (Ec2, S3, Vpc, Iam)

questions.explanation: An IAM Role can be attached to an EC2 instance via an instance profile to provide temporary credentials, avoiding long-term access keys on the host.
questions.question: Which AWS IAM entity is best suited for granting permissions to an application running on an EC2 instance without storing long-term access keys on the server?
questions.topic: Security & Identity (Iam, Kms, Secrets Management, Zero Trust)

questions.explanation: A Dockerfile contains instructions (FROM, RUN, COPY, etc.) that define how a Docker image is built.

options.B: To define how to build a container image
options.C: To orchestrate multiple containers across nodes
options.D: To encrypt container images at rest

questions.question: In Docker, what is the primary purpose of a Dockerfile?

questions.explanation: The top command provides a real-time view of system resource usage and running processes.
questions.question: In Linux, which command is commonly used to view real-time CPU and memory usage by processes?

questions.explanation: A NAT Gateway allows instances in private subnets to initiate outbound internet connections while preventing unsolicited inbound connections.
questions.question: You need private subnets to access the internet for OS updates without allowing inbound internet traffic. Which AWS component enables this pattern?
questions.topic: Aws Core Services (Ec2, S3, Vpc, Iam)

questions.explanation: Terraform state tracks resource IDs and attributes so Terraform can plan changes and manage real infrastructure safely and consistently.

options.A: To store application secrets for runtime use
options.B: To track the mapping between Terraform resources and real infrastructure objects
options.C: To compile HCL into machine code for faster execution
options.D: To store container images used by deployments

questions.question: In Terraform, what is the main purpose of the state file?

questions.explanation: OpenTelemetry provides standardized tracing instrumentation and the Collector can receive/process/export telemetry to multiple backends.

options.A: Enable CloudWatch Logs only and parse logs for trace IDs
options.B: Use OpenTelemetry instrumentation and an OpenTelemetry Collector to export traces
options.C: Rely solely on Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards
options.D: Use KMS to encrypt service-to-service traffic

questions.question: You want end-to-end request tracing across microservices running on Kubernetes and also want to export traces to multiple backends. Which approach best fits this requirement?

questions.explanation: Multi-AZ Auto Scaling behind an ALB distributes traffic across AZs and can replace failed instances, enabling AZ fault tolerance.

options.A: Single EC2 instance in one AZ with an Elastic IP
options.B: Auto Scaling group spanning multiple AZs behind an Application Load Balancer
options.C: Multiple EC2 instances in one AZ behind a Network Load Balancer
options.D: Run the application only on AWS Lambda without any networking configuration

questions.question: A production web application must tolerate the loss of an entire Availability Zone with minimal downtime. Which architecture best meets this requirement on AWS?
questions.topic: High Availability & Disaster Recovery (Multi-Az/Region, Backups, Rto/Rpo)

questions.explanation: Zero Trust assumes no implicit trust based on network location; it requires least privilege, strong identity, segmentation, and continuous verification (often with mTLS and policy enforcement).

options.A: Allow all east-west traffic and focus only on perimeter firewall rules
options.B: Implement least-privilege network controls (security groups/network policies), strong identity-based access, and continuous verification for service-to-service calls
options.C: Disable TLS internally to reduce latency and rely on VPC isolation
options.D: Use a single shared IAM user for all services to simplify auditing

questions.question: Your company is adopting Zero Trust. A team proposes allowing all traffic within the VPC because it is 'internal.' What is the best corrective action aligned with Zero Trust principles?
questions.topic: Security & Identity (Iam, Kms, Secrets Management, Zero Trust)

questions.explanation: IRSA provides pod-level, least-privilege access using short-lived credentials via OIDC, avoiding static keys in images, ConfigMaps, or nodes.

options.A: Store AWS access keys in a ConfigMap and mount it into the pod
options.B: Bake AWS access keys into the container image at build time
options.C: Use IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA) to map a Kubernetes service account to an IAM role
options.D: SSH into worker nodes and export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY as environment variables

questions.question: A Kubernetes workload on EKS needs to access an AWS API (e.g., S3) without using static credentials. Which solution is the most secure and cloud-native?
